rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s hearsay objections to the 3M documents are sustained. The 3M custodian was unable to provide any information about the person(s) who either wrote or provided the information about defendant¿s asserted supply of asbestos to 3M¿s facility, much less that such person(s) had knowledge of the relevant facts from personal observation. (Jazayeri v. Mao (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 301, 322 (¿The key to establishing the admissibility of a document made in the regular course of business is proof that the person who wrote the information or provided it had knowledge of the facts from personal observation.¿)). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Trane US Inc.¿s amended motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)
